title: RSA migration: C shims for PSS sign/verify
description:
Add wolfcrypt_rsa_pss_* C shims to compat_shim.c for RSA-PSS signature operations. Replaces EVP_PKEY_CTX + RSA_PKCS1_PSS_PADDING usage.

Functions to implement (guarded by #ifndef NO_RSA):
- wolfcrypt_rsa_pss_sign(void* ctx, int hash_type, int salt_len, const byte* msg, word32 msg_len, byte* sig, word32* sig_len)
  - Hash msg internally
  - wc_RsaPSS_Sign_ex with the given hash type and saltLen
- wolfcrypt_rsa_pss_verify(void* ctx, int hash_type, int salt_len, const byte* msg, word32 msg_len, const byte* sig, word32 sig_len)
  - Hash msg internally
  - wc_RsaPSS_VerifyCheck_ex with explicit saltLen
  - saltLen=-1 means RSA_PSS_SALT_LEN_DISCOVER (let wolfCrypt parse from encoding)
  - saltLen=-2 means use hash length (common default)

WC_RNG requirement: PSS signing requires a RNG for the salt. The ctx struct (from key lifecycle shim) must embed a WC_RNG, already initialized by wolfcrypt_rsa_new.

Acceptance criteria:
- PSS sign + verify round-trip succeeds
- Verify correctly rejects a tampered signature
- saltLen=-2 (hash-length salt) is tested as it is the most common production use
